title: Actinocephalus geniculatus
taxonomy: Actinocephalus geniculatus is classified as a species within the family Eriocaulaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pipeworts or fairy cups. Actinocephalus geniculatus belongs to the genus Actinocephalus, which is a group of plants native to South America. The Eriocaulaceae family is further classified under the magnoliid clade, a group of flowering plant families that diverged from the common ancestor of other angiosperms. Actinocephalus geniculatus is a member of this group due to its characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Actinocephalus geniculatus, commonly known as 'Brazilian feather grass', is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, providing potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds and skin infections. Bioactive constituents of Actinocephalus geniculatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
